success ac1041c045d33a994 local_agent completed Here is a complete summary of the project at `/home/user/workspace`. --- ## 1. Project Type: Next.js App Router - **Next.js version**: `16.2.0-canary.41` (canary build) - **React version**: `19.1.0` with `react-dom 19.1.0` - **TypeScript**: Strict mode, ESNext modules, bundler module resolution - **Entry point**: `app/` directory at the project root (not `src/app`), confirming **App Router** usage. There is no `pages/` directory. - **Config**: `next.config.ts` exists but is effectively empty (an exported empty object). - **Testing**: `vitest` with `@vitejs/plugin-react` and `vite-tsconfig-paths` is configured. - **Path aliases**: `@/*` maps to the project root (`./*`). --- ## 2. How Posts Are Currently Handled There is **no existing post logic at all**. The app consists of exactly two source files: ### `/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx` A minimal root layout that sets metadata (title: "Posts", description: "Create and view posts") and renders children inside a bare `` and ``. ### `/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx` A single home page component that renders: ```tsx

Posts

Please implement a Server Action for creating posts with cache invalidation.

``` There are: - **No API routes** (no `route.ts` or `route.tsx` files) - **No server actions** (`'use server'` directive not found anywhere in the app) - **No post-related components** (no PostList, PostForm, PostCard, etc.) - **No database or data layer** (no `db/`, `lib/`, `services/`, or `models/` directories) - **No actions directory** (`app/actions/` does not exist) --- ## 3. Where Server Actions Are Defined **Nowhere.** No server actions exist. A grep for `'use server'` and `"use server"` across the entire `app/` directory returned zero results. The placeholder text on the page (`"Please implement a Server Action for creating posts with cache invalidation"`) confirms this is a template/starter project waiting for server actions to be written. --- ## 4. Cache Tag Usage **None yet.** A search for `revalidateTag`, `revalidatePath`, `unstable_cache`, `cacheTag`, and related Next.js caching APIs across the project's source files returned zero hits (only hits inside `node_modules/`, which are type definitions from Next.js itself). The Next.js `cache` module (`next/cache`) is available and exports `unstable_cache`, `revalidatePath`, `revalidateTag`, `cacheTag`, and `unstable_cacheTag` — all ready to be imported from the application code, but none are used yet. --- ## 5. How the Posts List Component Works **It does not exist.** There is no posts list component. The `app/page.tsx` merely renders a placeholder message asking for a server action and cache invalidation to be implemented. --- ## Summary This is a **bare-bones Next.js 16 App Router starter project** with TypeScript and Vitest, containing: - A root layout (`app/layout.tsx`) with metadata about "Posts" - A home page (`app/page.tsx`) with a placeholder message It has **no** server actions, **no** API routes, **no** post components, **no** data layer, **no** cache tag usage, and **no** business logic. Everything is yet to be built.
